intro: Idertia axillaris, also known as Idertia, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, native to tropical regions of West Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Idertia axillaris include essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Idertia axillaris include decoctions, infusions, and essential oil extracts, often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
